title: ufputext
text: ufputext Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field, allowing the data to exceed the field length. Explain this code short ufputext (unsigned char* field, unsigned char* buffer, short length); Copy Parameters field Name of a field in your component. To resolve ambiguity, you can qualify the field name with an entity name, for example, field . entity . Instead of the actual field name, you also use the internal bind name as returned by ugetbind . buffer Data to be copied to the field. This data does not have to be zero-terminated. length Number of bytes that should be copied from buffer to field . Use a negative value to indicate that more data is to follow (using additional calls to ufputc ). Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ufput, ufputi, ufputc, and ufputext Value Meaning -1 An error occurred. 0 All data was copied to the field . 1 There is more data to be copied to the field. Occurs when you have specified a negative Length value. Use Library: yrtl Description ufputext treats the data as RAW. It is largely equivalent to ufput , but it is possible for the data to exceed the field length. However, if the field data will be stored, exceeding the field length will lead to a store error later on. CAUTION: ufputext should NOT be used in conjunction with ufputc . Related Links ufput Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. ufputc Copy additional data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. ufputi Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field as if the switch /init were used. ugetbind Get the internal bind name of a Uniface field.
